verses.latin_vulgate: Cecineruntque Debbora et Barac filius Abinoëm in illo die, dicentes :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּ֣שַׁר דְּבוֹרָ֔ה וּבָרָ֖ק בֶּן־אֲבִינֹ֑עַם בַּיּ֥וֹם הַה֖וּא לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: In that day Debbora and Barac, son of Abinoem, sung, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin veisasi Debora ja Barak Abinoamin poika sinä päivänä, sanoen:
verses.king_james: Then sang Deborah and Barak the son of Abinoam on that day, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui sponte obtulistis de Israël animas vestras ad periculum,  benedicite Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּפְרֹ֤עַ פְּרָעוֹת֙ בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל בְּהִתְנַדֵּ֖ב עָ֑ם בָּֽרְכ֖וּ יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: O you of Israel, that have willingly offered your lives to danger, bless the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että Israelin puolesta on täydellisesti kostettu ja että kansa antoi itsensä mieluisesti siihen, sentähden kiittäkäät Herraa.
verses.king_james: Praise ye the Lord for the avenging of Israel, when the people willingly offered themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, reges ; auribus percipite, principes :  ego sum, ego sum, quæ Domino canam,  psallam Domino Deo Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֣וּ מְלָכִ֔ים הַאֲזִ֖ינוּ רֹֽזְנִ֑ים אָנֹכִ֗י לַֽיהֹוָה֙ אָנֹכִ֣י אָשִׁ֔ירָה אֲזַמֵּ֕ר לַֽיהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hear, O ye kings, give ear, O ye princes: It is I, it is I, that will sing to the Lord, I will sing to the Lord, the God of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuulkaat te kuninkaat ja ottakaat vaari te päämiehet: minä, minä veisaan Herralle, ja Herralle Israelin Jumalalle minä veisaan.
verses.king_james: Hear, O ye kings; give ear, O ye princes; I, even I, will sing unto the Lord; I will sing praise to the Lord God of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, cum exires de Seir,  et transires per regiones Edom,  terra mota est,  cælique ac nubes distillaverunt aquis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֗ה בְּצֵאתְךָ֤ מִשֵּׂעִיר֙ בְּצַעְדְּךָ֙ מִשְּׂדֵ֣ה אֱד֔וֹם אֶ֣רֶץ רָעָ֔שָׁה גַּם־שָׁמַ֖יִם נָטָ֑פוּ גַּם־עָבִ֖ים נָ֥טְפוּ מָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: O Lord, when thou wentest out of Seir, and passedst by the regions of Edom, the earth trembled, and the heavens and clouds dropped water.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, kuin sinä läksit Seiristä ja kävit Edomin kedolta, silloin maa vapisi, taivaat myös tiukkuivat, ja pilvet pisaroitsivat vettä.
verses.king_james: Lord, when thou wentest out of Seir, when thou marchedst out of the field of Edom, the earth trembled, and the heavens dropped, the clouds also dropped water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Montes fluxerunt a facie Domini,  et Sinai a facie Domini Dei Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָרִ֥ים נָזְל֖וּ מִפְּנֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֑ה זֶ֣ה סִינַ֔י מִפְּנֵ֕י יְהֹוָ֖ה אֱלֹהֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: The mountains melted before the face of the Lord, and Sinai before the face of the Lord the God of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vuoret vuosivat Herran edessä, itse Sinai Herran, Israelin Jumalan edessä.
verses.king_james: The mountains melted from before the Lord, even that Sinai from before the Lord God of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus Samgar filii Anath,  in diebus Jahel quieverunt semitæ :  et qui ingrediebantur per eas,  ambulaverunt per calles devios.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּימֵ֞י שַׁמְגַּ֤ר בֶּן־עֲנָת֙ בִּימֵ֣י יָעֵ֔ל חָֽדְל֖וּ אֳרָח֑וֹת וְהֹלְכֵ֣י נְתִיב֔וֹת יֵלְכ֕וּ אֳרָח֖וֹת עֲקַלְקַלּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the days of Samgar, the son of Anath, in the days of Jahel, the paths rested: and they that went by them, walked through bye-ways.
verses.finnish_biblia: Samgarin Anatin pojan aikana ja Jaelin aikana tiet katosivat, ja ne kuin kohdastansa käymän piti, kävivät vääriä polkuja.
verses.king_james: In the days of Shamgar the son of Anath, in the days of Jael, the highways were unoccupied, and the travellers walked through byways.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cessaverunt fortes in Israël, et quieverunt :  donec surgeret Debbora,  surgeret mater in Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָדְל֧וּ פְרָז֛וֹן בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל חָדֵ֑לּוּ עַ֤ד שַׁקַּ֙מְתִּי֙ דְּבוֹרָ֔ה שַׁקַּ֥מְתִּי אֵ֖ם בְּיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: The valiant men ceased, and rested in Israel: until Debbora arose, a mother arose in Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Talonpojat puuttuivat Israelista, ne puuttuivat siihenasti kuin minä Debora tulin, siihenasti kuin minä tulin äidiksi Israeliin.
verses.king_james: The inhabitants of the villages ceased, they ceased in Israel, until that I Deborah arose, that I arose a mother in Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nova bella elegit Dominus,  et portas hostium ipse subvertit :  clypeus et hasta si apparuerint  in quadraginta millibus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִבְחַר֙ אֱלֹהִ֣ים חֲדָשִׁ֔ים אָ֖ז לָחֶ֣ם שְׁעָרִ֑ים מָגֵ֤ן אִם־יֵֽרָאֶה֙ וָרֹ֔מַח בְּאַרְבָּעִ֥ים אֶ֖לֶף בְּיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord chose new wars, and he himself overthrew the gates of the enemies: a shield and spear was not seen among forty thousand of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän valitsi uusia jumalia: silloin oli sota porteissa: ei siellä nähty kilpeä eikä keihästä neljänkymmenen tuhannen seassa Israelissa.
verses.king_james: They chose new gods; then was war in the gates: was there a shield or spear seen among forty thousand in Israel?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor meum diligit principes Israël :  qui propria voluntate obtulistis vos discrimini,  benedicite Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִבִּי֙ לְחוֹקְקֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל הַמִּֽתְנַדְּבִ֖ים בָּעָ֑ם בָּרְכ֖וּ יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: My heart loveth the princes of Israel: O you, that of your own good will offered yourselves to danger, bless the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun sydämelläni on ilo Israelin valtamiehistä: he ovat hyväntahtoiset kansan seassa; kiittäkäät Herraa.
verses.king_james: My heart is toward the governors of Israel, that offered themselves willingly among the people. Bless ye the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ascenditis super nitentes asinos,  et sedetis in judicio,  et ambulatis in via,  loquimini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹכְבֵי֩ אֲתֹנ֨וֹת צְחֹר֜וֹת יֹשְׁבֵ֧י עַל־מִדִּ֛ין וְהֹלְכֵ֥י עַל־דֶּ֖רֶךְ שִֽׂיחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Speak, you that ride upon fair asses, and you that sit in judgment, and walk in the way.
verses.finnish_biblia: Te jotka ajatte kaunisten aasein päällä, te jotka istutte oikeudessa, te jotka käytte teitä myöten, puhukaat näistä.
verses.king_james: Speak, ye that ride on white asses, ye that sit in judgment, and walk by the way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi collisi sunt currus,  et hostium suffocatus est exercitus,  ibi narrentur justitiæ Domini,  et clementia in fortes Israël :  tunc descendit populus Domini ad portas,  et obtinuit principatum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִקּ֣וֹל מְחַֽצְצִ֗ים בֵּ֚ין מַשְׁאַבִּ֔ים שָׁ֤ם יְתַנּוּ֙ צִדְק֣וֹת יְהֹוָ֔ה צִדְקֹ֥ת פִּרְזוֹנ֖וֹ בְּיִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל אָ֛ז יָֽרְד֥וּ לַשְּׁעָרִ֖ים עַם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where the chariots were dashed together, and the army of the enemies was choked, there let the justices of the Lord be rehearsed, and his clemency towards the brave men of Israel: then the people of the Lord went down to the gates, and obtained the sovereignty.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kussa ampujat huusivat vedenkantajain seassa, siellä julistettakaan Herran vanhurskautta ja hänen talonpoikainsa vanhurskautta Israelissa; silloin meni Herran kansa porttein tykö.
verses.king_james: They that are delivered from the noise of archers in the places of drawing water, there shall they rehearse the righteous acts of the Lord, even the righteous acts toward the inhabitants of his villages in Israel: then shall the people of the Lord go down to the gates.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surge, surge Debbora ;  surge, surge, et loquere canticum :  surge Barac, et apprehende captivos tuos, fili Abinoëm.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עוּרִ֤י עוּרִי֙ דְּבוֹרָ֔ה ע֥וּרִי ע֖וּרִי דַּבְּרִי־שִׁ֑יר ק֥וּם בָּרָ֛ק וּֽשְׁבֵ֥ה שֶׁבְיְךָ֖ בֶּן־אֲבִינֹֽעַם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arise, arise, O Debbora, arise, arise, and utter a canticle. Arise, Barac, and take hold of thy captives, O son of Abinoem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nouse, nouse, Debora: nouse, nouse ja veisaa virsiä, valmista itses, Barak, vangiksi ota sinun vangitsiat, sinä Abinoamin poika.
verses.king_james: Awake, awake, Deborah: awake, awake, utter a song: arise, Barak, and lead thy captivity captive, thou son of Abinoam.

verses.latin_vulgate: Salvatæ sunt reliquiæ populi :  Dominus in fortibus dimicavit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֚ז יְרַ֣ד שָׂרִ֔יד לְאַדִּירִ֖ים עָ֑ם יְהֹוָ֕ה יְרַד־לִ֖י בַּגִּבּוֹרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: The remnants of the people are saved, the Lord hath fought among the valiant ones.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin rupesi se jäänyt vallitsemaan voimallista kansaa: Herra on hallinnut väkeviä minun kauttani.
verses.king_james: Then he made him that remaineth have dominion over the nobles among the people: the Lord made me have dominion over the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex Ephraim delevit eos in Amalec,  et post eum ex Benjamin in populos tuos, o Amalec :  de Machir principes descenderunt,  et de Zabulon qui exercitum ducerent ad bellandum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִנִּ֣י אֶפְרַ֗יִם שׇׁרְשָׁם֙ בַּעֲמָלֵ֔ק אַחֲרֶ֥יךָ בִנְיָמִ֖ין בַּעֲמָמֶ֑יךָ מִנִּ֣י מָכִ֗יר יָֽרְדוּ֙ מְחֹ֣קְקִ֔ים וּמִ֨זְּבוּלֻ֔ן מֹשְׁכִ֖ים בְּשֵׁ֥בֶט סֹפֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Out of Ephraim he destroyed them into Amalec, and after him out of Benjamin into thy people, O Amalec: Out of Machir there came down princes, and out of Zabulon they that led the army to fight.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ephraimista oli heidän juurensa Amalekia vastaan, ja sinun jälkees BenJamin sinun kansoissas; Makirista ovat tulleet hallitsiat ja Sebulonista kirjoittajat.
verses.king_james: Out of Ephraim was there a root of them against Amalek; after thee, Benjamin, among thy people; out of Machir came down governors, and out of Zebulun they that handle the pen of the writer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duces Issachar fuere cum Debbora,  et Barac vestigia sunt secuti,  qui quasi in præceps ac barathrum se discrimini dedit :  diviso contra se Ruben,  magnanimorum reperta est contentio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשָׂרַ֤י בְּיִשָּׂשכָר֙ עִם־דְּבֹרָ֔ה וְיִשָּׂשכָר֙ כֵּ֣ן בָּרָ֔ק בָּעֵ֖מֶק שֻׁלַּ֣ח בְּרַגְלָ֑יו בִּפְלַגּ֣וֹת רְאוּבֵ֔ן גְּדֹלִ֖ים חִקְקֵי־לֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: The captains of Issachar were with Debbora, and followed the steps of Barac, who exposed himself to danger, as one going headlong, and into a pit. Ruben being divided against himself, there was found a strife of courageous men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Isaskarin päämiehet olivat myös Deboran kanssa, ja Isaskar oli niinkuin Barak laaksoon lähetetty jalkaväkinensä; Rubenin eroituksessa ovat suuret sydämen ajatukset.
verses.king_james: And the princes of Issachar were with Deborah; even Issachar, and also Barak: he was sent on foot into the valley. For the divisions of Reuben there were great thoughts of heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare habitas inter duos terminos,  ut audias sibilos gregum ?  diviso contra se Ruben,  magnanimorum reperta est contentio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֣מָּה יָשַׁ֗בְתָּ בֵּ֚ין הַֽמִּשְׁפְּתַ֔יִם לִשְׁמֹ֖עַ שְׁרִק֣וֹת עֲדָרִ֑ים לִפְלַגּ֣וֹת רְאוּבֵ֔ן גְּדוֹלִ֖ים חִקְרֵי־לֵֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why dwellest thou between two borders, that thou mayst hear the bleatings of the flocks? Ruben being divided against himself, there was found a strife of courageous men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksis istuit kahden joukon välillä, kuullakses laumain määkynätä? Rubenin eroituksessa ovat suuret sydämen ajatukset.
verses.king_james: Why abodest thou among the sheepfolds, to hear the bleatings of the flocks? For the divisions of Reuben there were great searchings of heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Galaad trans Jordanem quiescebat,  et Dan vacabat navibus :  Aser habitabat in littore maris,  et in portubus morabatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גִּלְעָ֗ד בְּעֵ֤בֶר הַיַּרְדֵּן֙ שָׁכֵ֔ן וְדָ֕ן לָ֥מָּה יָג֖וּר אֳנִיּ֑וֹת אָשֵׁ֗ר יָשַׁב֙ לְח֣וֹף יַמִּ֔ים וְעַ֥ל מִפְרָצָ֖יו יִשְׁכּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Galaad rested beyond the Jordan, and Dan applied himself to ships: Aser dwelt on the sea shore, and abode in the havens.
verses.finnish_biblia: Gilead jäi toiselle puolelle Jordania, ja miksi Dan asui haahtein seassa? Asser istui meren satamissa ja asui langenneissa kylissänsä.
verses.king_james: Gilead abode beyond Jordan: and why did Dan remain in ships? Asher continued on the sea shore, and abode in his breaches.

verses.latin_vulgate: Zabulon vero et Nephthali obtulerunt animas suas morti  in regione Merome.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְבֻל֗וּן עַ֣ם חֵרֵ֥ף נַפְשׁ֛וֹ לָמ֖וּת וְנַפְתָּלִ֑י עַ֖ל מְרוֹמֵ֥י שָׂדֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But Zabulon and Nephthali offered their lives to death in the region of Merome.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sebulonin kansa antoi henkensä alttiiksi kuolemaan, Naphtali myös, kedon korkeudella.
verses.king_james: Zebulun and Naphtali were a people that jeoparded their lives unto the death in the high places of the field.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venerunt reges et pugnaverunt :  pugnaverunt reges Chanaan  in Thanach juxta aquas Mageddo,  et tamen nihil tulere prædantes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֤אוּ מְלָכִים֙ נִלְחָ֔מוּ אָ֤ז נִלְחֲמוּ֙ מַלְכֵ֣י כְנַ֔עַן בְּתַעְנַ֖ךְ עַל־מֵ֣י מְגִדּ֑וֹ בֶּ֥צַע כֶּ֖סֶף לֹ֥א לָקָֽחוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The kings came and fought, the kings of Chanaan fought in Thanac, by the waters of Mageddo and yet they took no spoils.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuninkaat tulivat ja sotivat; silloin sotivat Kanaanin kuninkaat Taanakissa Megiddon veden tykönä: ei he saaneet rahaa saaliiksensa.
verses.king_james: The kings came and fought, then fought the kings of Canaan in Taanach by the waters of Megiddo; they took no gain of money.

verses.latin_vulgate: De cælo dimicatum est contra eos :  stellæ manentes in ordine et cursu suo,  adversus Sisaram pugnaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִן־שָׁמַ֖יִם נִלְחָ֑מוּ הַכּֽוֹכָבִים֙ מִמְּסִלּוֹתָ֔ם נִלְחֲמ֖וּ עִם־סִֽיסְרָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: There was war made against them from heaven: the stars, remaining in their order and courses, fought against Sisara.
verses.finnish_biblia: Taivaasta sotivat tähdet juoksussansa, he sotivat Siseraa vastaan.
verses.king_james: They fought from heaven; the stars in their courses fought against Sisera.

verses.latin_vulgate: Torrens Cison traxit cadavera eorum,  torrens Cadumim, torrens Cison :  conculca, anima mea, robustos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נַ֤חַל קִישׁוֹן֙ גְּרָפָ֔ם נַ֥חַל קְדוּמִ֖ים נַ֣חַל קִישׁ֑וֹן תִּדְרְכִ֥י נַפְשִׁ֖י עֹֽז׃
verses.douay_rheims: The torrent of Cison dragged their carcasses, the torrent of Cadumim, the torrent of Cison: tread thou, my soul, upon the strong ones.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kisonin ojan kieritti heitä, Kedumin oja, Kisonin oja; minun sieluni tallaa väkeviä.
verses.king_james: The river of Kishon swept them away, that ancient river, the river Kishon. O my soul, thou hast trodden down strength.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ungulæ equorum ceciderunt, fugientibus impetu,  et per præceps ruentibus fortissimis hostium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֥ז הָלְמ֖וּ עִקְּבֵי־ס֑וּס מִֽדַּהֲר֖וֹת דַּהֲר֥וֹת אַבִּירָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: The hoofs of the horses were broken whilst the stoutest of the enemies fled amain, and fell headlong down.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin sotkuivat hevosten kaviot heitä, heidän väkeväinsä hätäisessä paossa.
verses.king_james: Then were the horsehoofs broken by the means of the pransings, the pransings of their mighty ones.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maledicite terræ Meroz, dixit angelus Domini :  maledicite habitatoribus ejus,  quia non venerunt ad auxilium Domini,  in adjutorium fortissimorum ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֣וֹרוּ מֵר֗וֹז אָמַר֙ מַלְאַ֣ךְ יְהֹוָ֔ה אֹ֥רוּ אָר֖וֹר יֹשְׁבֶ֑יהָ כִּ֤י לֹֽא־בָ֙אוּ֙ לְעֶזְרַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֔ה לְעֶזְרַ֥ת יְהֹוָ֖ה בַּגִּבּוֹרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Curse ye the land of Meroz, said the angel of the Lord: curse the inhabitants thereof, because they came not to the help of the Lord, to help his most valiant men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kirotkaat Merosta, sanoi Herran enkeli, kiroten kirotkaat hänen asuvaisiansa, ettei he tulleet Herran avuksi, Herran avuksi sankarien seassa.
verses.king_james: Curse ye Meroz, said the angel of the Lord, curse ye bitterly the inhabitants thereof; because they came not to the help of the Lord, to the help of the Lord against the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Benedicta inter mulieres Jahel uxor Haber Cinæi,  et benedicatur in tabernaculo suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּבֹרַךְ֙ מִנָּשִׁ֔ים יָעֵ֕ל אֵ֖שֶׁת חֶ֣בֶר הַקֵּינִ֑י מִנָּשִׁ֥ים בָּאֹ֖הֶל תְּבֹרָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Blessed among women be Jahel, the wife of Haber the Cinite, and blessed be she in her tent.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siunattu olkoon Jael vaimoin seassa, Heberin Keniläisen emäntä, siunattu olkoon hän majoissansa vaimoin seassa.
verses.king_james: Blessed above women shall Jael the wife of Heber the Kenite be, blessed shall she be above women in the tent.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aquam petenti lac dedit,  et in phiala principum obtulit butyrum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֥יִם שָׁאַ֖ל חָלָ֣ב נָתָ֑נָה בְּסֵ֥פֶל אַדִּירִ֖ים הִקְרִ֥יבָה חֶמְאָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He asked her water, and she gave him milk, and offered him butter in a dish fit for princes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin hän vettä anoi, niin hän rieskaa antoi; hän kantoi voita kalliissa maljassa.
verses.king_james: He asked water, and she gave him milk; she brought forth butter in a lordly dish.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sinistram manum misit ad clavum,  et dexteram ad fabrorum malleos.  Percussitque Sisaram quærens in capite vulneri locum,  et tempus valide perforans :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָדָהּ֙ לַיָּתֵ֣ד תִּשְׁלַ֔חְנָה וִימִינָ֖הּ לְהַלְמ֣וּת עֲמֵלִ֑ים וְהָלְמָ֤ה סִֽיסְרָא֙ מָחֲקָ֣ה רֹאשׁ֔וֹ וּמָחֲצָ֥ה וְחָלְפָ֖ה רַקָּתֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She put her left hand to the nail, and her right hand to the workman's hammer, and she struck Sisara, seeking in his head a place for the wound, and strongly piercing through his temples.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän otti naulan käteensä ja sepän vasaran oikiaan käteensä, ja löi Siseran ja runteli hänen päänsä, musersi ja lävisti hänen korvainsa juuren.
verses.king_james: She put her hand to the nail, and her right hand to the workmen’s hammer; and with the hammer she smote Sisera, she smote off his head, when she had pierced and stricken through his temples.

verses.latin_vulgate: inter pedes ejus ruit ; defecit, et mortuus est :  volvebatur ante pedes ejus,  et jacebat exanimis et miserabilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּ֣ין רַגְלֶ֔יהָ כָּרַ֥ע נָפַ֖ל שָׁכָ֑ב בֵּ֤ין רַגְלֶ֙יהָ֙ כָּרַ֣ע נָפָ֔ל בַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר כָּרַ֔ע שָׁ֖ם נָפַ֥ל שָׁדֽוּד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Between her feet he fell: he fainted, and he died: he rolled before her feet, and there he lay lifeless and wretched.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen jalkainsa juureen kumarsi hän maahan, hän lankesi maahan ja pani maata: hänen jalkainsa juureen kumarsi hän itsensä ja lankesi maahan, siinä kuin hän kumarsi itsensä, siinä hän myös makasi hukutettuna.
verses.king_james: At her feet he bowed, he fell, he lay down: at her feet he bowed, he fell: where he bowed, there he fell down dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per fenestram respiciens, ululabat mater ejus :  et de cœnaculo loquebatur :  Cur moratur regredi currus ejus ?  quare tardaverunt pedes quadrigarum illius ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּעַד֩ הַחַלּ֨וֹן נִשְׁקְפָ֧ה וַתְּיַבֵּ֛ב אֵ֥ם סִֽיסְרָ֖א בְּעַ֣ד הָאֶשְׁנָ֑ב מַדּ֗וּעַ בֹּשֵׁ֤שׁ רִכְבּוֹ֙ לָב֔וֹא מַדּ֣וּעַ אֶֽחֱר֔וּ פַּעֲמֵ֖י מַרְכְּבוֹתָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: His mother looked out at a window, and howled: and she spoke from the dining room: Why is his chariot so long in coming back? Why are the feet of his horses so slow?
verses.finnish_biblia: Siseran äiti katsoi akkunasta ja itki huutain häkistä: miksi viipyy hänen vaununsa tulemasta? miksi ovat hänen vaununsa pyörät niin jääneet jälkeen?
verses.king_james: The mother of Sisera looked out at a window, and cried through the lattice, Why is his chariot so long in coming? why tarry the wheels of his chariots?

verses.latin_vulgate: Una sapientior ceteris uxoribus ejus,  hæc socrui verba respondit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַכְמ֥וֹת שָׂרוֹתֶ֖יהָ תַּעֲנֶ֑ינָּה אַף־הִ֕יא תָּשִׁ֥יב אֲמָרֶ֖יהָ לָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: One that was wiser than the rest of his wives, returned this answer to her mother in law:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ne viisaimmat hänen ylimmäisistä vaimoistansa vastasivat häntä, että hän sanojansa kertoi.
verses.king_james: Her wise ladies answered her, yea, she returned answer to herself,

verses.latin_vulgate: Forsitan nunc dividit spolia,  et pulcherrima feminarum eligitur ei :  vestes diversorum colorum Sisaræ traduntur in prædam,  et supellex varia ad ornanda colla congeritur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹ֨א יִמְצְא֜וּ יְחַלְּק֣וּ שָׁלָ֗ל רַ֤חַם רַחֲמָתַ֙יִם֙ לְרֹ֣אשׁ גֶּ֔בֶר שְׁלַ֤ל צְבָעִים֙ לְסִ֣יסְרָ֔א שְׁלַ֥ל צְבָעִ֖ים רִקְמָ֑ה צֶ֥בַע רִקְמָתַ֖יִם לְצַוְּארֵ֥י שָׁלָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Perhaps he is now dividing the spoils, and the fairest of the women is chosen out for him: garments of divers colours are given to Sisara for his prey, and furniture of different kinds is heaped together to adorn necks.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö he löydä ja jaa saalista, jokaiselle miehelle piian taikka kaksi, ja Siseralle kirjavia ja ommeltuja vaatteita, neulalla ommeltuja vaatteita, kirjavia molemmin puolin kaulan ympäri, saaliiksi.
verses.king_james: Have they not sped? have they not divided the prey; to every man a damsel or two; to Sisera a prey of divers colours, a prey of divers colours of needlework, of divers colours of needlework on both sides, meet for the necks of them that take the spoil?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic pereant omnes inimici tui, Domine :  qui autem diligunt te, sicut sol in ortu suo splendet, ita rutilent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֵּ֠ן יֹאבְד֤וּ כׇל־אוֹיְבֶ֙יךָ֙ יְהֹוָ֔ה וְאֹ֣הֲבָ֔יו כְּצֵ֥את הַשֶּׁ֖מֶשׁ בִּגְבֻרָת֑וֹ וַתִּשְׁקֹ֥ט הָאָ֖רֶץ אַרְבָּעִ֥ים שָׁנָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: So let all thy enemies perish, O Lord: but let them that love thee shine, as the sun shineth in his rising.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki sinun vihollises niin hukkukoon, Herra! Mutta ne, kuin sinua rakastavat, olkoon niinkuin ylöskäypä aurinko voimassansa. Ja maa oli levossa neljäkymmentä ajastaikaa.
verses.king_james: So let all thine enemies perish, O Lord: but let them that love him be as the sun when he goeth forth in his might. And the land had rest forty years.
